page.content: In the analysis of the powertrain mix evolution for Stellantis as of December 2023, the Powertrain Type categories reflect significant financial and market metrics: Internal Combustion at $5,000M with a YoY Change of 5%, holding a Market Share of 40% and Unit Sales of 20,000; Electric, valued at $3,000M with a YoY Change of 15%, capturing 25% Market Share and 10,000 Unit Sales; Hybrid, at $2,000M with a 10% YoY Change, representing a 20% Market Share and 8,000 Unit Sales; Plug-in Hybrid, priced at $1,500M with a 20% YoY Change, accounting for 15% Market Share and 5,000 Unit Sales; and Total Powertrain aggregates to $11,500M with an 8% YoY Change, 100% Market Share, and 45,000 Unit Sales.

page.content: Future forecasts indicate $6,000M for 2024 with a 10% YoY Change and 42% Market Share at 22,000 Unit Sales, $7,000M for 2025 with an 8% YoY Change and 45% Market Share at 25,000 Unit Sales, and $8,000M for 2026 with a 7% YoY Change and 48% Market Share at 28,000 Unit Sales.

page.content: In the battery cost analysis, Lithium-ion costs $150 per kWh for 2023 with N/A for Change and forecasts to 2024; Projected Costs for 2025 are $140 with N/A for Change and forecasts to 2026; Solid-State batteries are $200 in 2023 with N/A for Change, forecasted to drop to $180 in 2024; Lead-Acid costs $100 in 2023 with N/A for Change, forecasting $90 in 2024; Nickel-Metal Hydride stands at $120 for 2023 with N/A for Change and forecasting $110 in 2024; and the Total Average battery cost is $140 for 2023 with N/A for Change, forecasting $130 in 2024.

Nickel-Metal Hydride stands at $120 for 2023 with N/A for Change and forecasting $110 in 2024; and the Total Average battery
cost is $140 for 2023 with N/A for Change, forecasting $130 in 2024.
